today_news.title: The Download: how the World Cup ball will fly and OpenAI’s “super app”
today_news.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. today_news.title: Microsoft Research's Lens proves detailed captions matter more than raw scale for training efficient image generators
today_news.summary: Microsoft Research presents Lens, a text-to-image model with just 3.8 billion parameters that matches much larger rivals on benchmarks, at a fraction of the training cost. The secret sauce: 800 million detailed image captions generated by GPT-4.1 instead of vague web alt-text. Code and weights are o...

today_news.title: Instagram AI chatbot breach may have affected over to 20,000 accounts, Meta discloses
today_news.summary: Meta has put a number on the security breach in its AI support chatbot for Instagram for the first time: at least 20,225 accounts were compromised. For nearly seven weeks, the system sent password reset links to arbitrary email addresses without verifying they belonged to the account. The chatbot ha...

today_news.title: Frontier Radar #3: How agentic AI is turning tokens into a business metric
today_news.summary: Monthly subscription, open chat, ask question: This is how generative AI used to work. Agentic workflows go beyond this model. They consume many times more tokens, run autonomously for hours and make flat rates unaffordable for providers. At the same time, token prices vary according to speed, speci...

today_news.title: Intel gets a second life as Google and Nvidia explore it as a TSMC backup for AI chips
today_news.summary: Google has ordered more than three million AI chips from Intel for 2028. Nvidia is testing Intel's manufacturing tech for its upcoming Feynman architecture. Both moves come as TSMC can't keep up with AI chip demand. Intel's long-struggling foundry division is getting a rare second chance.

today_news.title: Most companies are flying blind on AI spending
today_news.summary: Only 26 percent of companies have full visibility into their AI costs, a KPMG survey finds.

today_tech.title: OmniMem: Perturbation-aware Memory Compression for Streaming Audio-Visual LLMs
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Audio-visual large language models (LLMs) hold strong promise for long-form video understanding, yet their long-video inference is fundamentally limited by the linear growth of video tokens and key-value (KV) caches. today_tech.title: Offline Reinforcement Learning for Plasma Control in Nuclear Fusion: Codebase and Benchmark
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) offers a promising route for developing plasma controllers from historical tokamak data, since online trial-and-error on real devices is costly and risky.
